Farn Polrrrcel PnecrrcEs CouvrrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
August 19,2004
Leslie E. Murad, II
Office of the City Attorney
City of Redlands
Post Office Box 3005
Redlands, CA 92373-1505
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-04-067
Dear Mr. Murad:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of the city council for the City
of Redlands regarding Ralph Megna for advice regardingthe conflict-ot interest and
filing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the ..Act,).r
QUESiTON
For the pulposes of qualification as a consultant under the city's conflict of
interest code, does the principal of Inland Empire Solutions, Ralph M"gnu, quali$z as a
"consultant" to the City of Redlands under the Act?
CONCLUSION
No. Based on the information you have provided, Mr. Megna is not considered a
"consultant" to the City of Redlands under the Act. In the event that Mr. Megna's duties
change, however, you should seek further advice. However, in accordance w]th the city,s
conflict of interest code, this determination should be made by the city council regarding
whether Mr. Megna is a consultant under the city's conflict of interesi code.
FACTS
To assist the city council and the redevelopment agency's board of directors, the
city has contracted with the Redlands Chamber of Commerce, a nonprofit trade
association, for the chamber's retention of a consultant to .rr"or.ug" business
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}14. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.


FileNo. A-04-067
Page No. 2
development within the city. The city, redevelopment agancy and chamber of commerce
desire to create an economic development program which focuses on redevelopment,
recruitment, retention and growth of businesses within the city.
The conkact provides that the chamber will hire an economic development
consultant with proven experience in municipal redevelopment, business recruitment,
expansion and/or retention of businesses. In exchange for tfre chamber hiring the
consultant, the city pays the chamber $90,000 arrrr,rully to be used, in part, toward the
salary of the consultant. The chamber provides office space and staffsupport for the
consultant's activities, and is required to update the citywith written and oral quarterly
reports detailing the consultant's activities in redevelopmen! business recruitrnent,
expansion and retention.
The city also provides support to the consultant. The city manager, city staffand
the consultant engage in regular meetings in which business developmit is discussed,
including financial assistance to businesses along with the possibleicquisition and
disposition of properti es.
The chamber hired Empire Development Solutions and its principal, Ralph
Megna, as the economic development consultant to coordinate economic development
activities with the city and the redevelopment agency. The consultant provides
information, recornmendations and advice to thi city council and the redevelopment
agency's board, the cit5r's business and economic development commiuee and city staff.
It is the city's belief that the consultant is performing substantially the same duties as an
economic development director wouldprovide to the city and redlvelopment agency if
that position was authorized and filled by an employee of the city. Neiiher the city nor
the agency's conflict of interest code designatesl'economic development consultant,, as a
position. "Consultant," however, is listed as a designated positionin both conflict of
interest codes with the city council making the determination ofwho qualifies as a
consultant under the conflict of interest code.2
In a telephone conversation on April 2,2004,you added that the duration of the
contract is one year- You also stated that Mr. Megna would only make recommendations
to the city council and the redevelopment agencyt board, the city's business and
economic development committee and city staff, but would not make any decisions.
ANALYSIS
The Act's conflict-of-interest rules prohibit a public official from makjng,
participating in making, or using his or her official positio, in any way to influence a
governmental decision in which the official knows, or has reason to know, that he or she
has a "financial interest." (Section 87100.) Section 87103 provides that a public official
has a "financial interest" in a govemmental decision "if it is reasonably foieseeable that
the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable fromlts effect on the
public generally, on the official, a mernber of his or her immediate family,, or on any of
' This information was provided by you in a telephone discussion on August lg,20f/'.


FileNo. A-04-067
Page No. 3
the official's economic interests. In addition, state and local public officials must file
periodic statements of economic interests (Form 700) disclosing those personal assets and
interests which may be affected during the performance of theiiofficiai duties. (Sections
87200 - 87350.)
Your request for advice on behalf of the Redlands City Council relates to the
interpretation of the city's conflict of interest code. The city council determines who
qualifies as a consultant under that code and is the code reviewing body as well. Where
the Commission is not the code reviewing body for the conflict of interest code of the
agency or individual questioning the conflict of interest code interpretation, the
Commission provides advice only in situations where the individual or agency has
alteady requested an interpretation from the code reviewing body- (Regulation
18329.5(a)(3).) In this case, the code reviewing body is requesting it " Co**ission,s
interpretation in coordination with the agency and individuals in qirestion, so the
Commission is able to provide advice.
The Act defines "public official" to include "everymember, officer, employee or
eonsultant of a state or local government agency." (Section Sz}4l,emphasis uaa"a.;
The local agency sponsors referred to in this advice request me by definition ..local
government agencies" under section 82041. Regulation 18701 provides forpurposes of
section 82048 (and section82019 which defines "designated employee,), th; definition
of a "consultant":
"(2) 'Consultant'means an individual who, pursuant to a
contract with a state or local government agency:
(A) Makes a govemmental decision whether to:
l. Approve arate, rule, orregulation;
2. Adopt or enforce alaw;
3. Issue, deny, suspend, or revoke any permit, license,
application, certificate, approval, order, or similar
authorization or entitlement;
4. Authorize the agency to enter into, modify, or renew a
contract provided it is the type of contract that requires
agency approval;
5. Grant agency approval to a contract that requires agency
approval and to which the agency is a party, or to the
specifications for such a contract;
6. Grant agency approval to a plan, design, report, study, or
similar item;


FileNo. A-04-067
Page No. 4
7 - Adopt, or grant agency approvar o{ poricies, standards, or
guidelines for the agency, or for any subdivision thereof; or
(B) serves in a staff capacity with the agency and in that
capacrty participates in making a governmental decision as
defined in Regulation 19702.2 orperforms the same or
substantially all the same duties for the agency that would
otherwise be performed by an individual holding a position
specified in the agency's conflict of Interest code under
Govemment Code Section g73OZ.- (Regulation
18701(a)e).)
We note at the outset that abusiness (such as Empire Development Solutions) or
goveflrmental entity cannot be a "consultant" under regulation ttlOilallZ), since the
term is applied only to an "individual," thatis, to a r,uto*l person. y;;*t 
look to the
person performing the duties under the contract to determine who, if anyong is acting as
a consultant- (Sanchez Advice Letter, No. I-03-173.) You have stated that Mr. Meg;a
will be performing all of the duties pursuant to the contract between Empire
Development Solutions and the City of Redlands and the city council is attempting to
determine if IvIr. Megna is a consultant under the Act.
Regulation 18701(a)Q) establishes two standards for qualification as a consultant.
An individual who satisfies either standard is a consultant foithepurposes ofthe Act.
First, an individual may be a "consultant" if he or she performs, p".arr*t to a contract,
any of the actions described in subdivisions (a)(2)(A)tf >fZl of regulation lg71l.
Altematively, an individual may be a consultant ifhe ii she "servk in staffcapacity with
the agency'' under subdivision (a)(2)(B).
Based on the facts provided, the duties performed under the contract by Mr.
Megna include coordinating economic development activities with the city and the
redevelopment agency and providing information, recoillmendations and advice to the
city council, the redevelopment agency's board, the city,s business and economic
development committee and city staff. However, you *tut" thut Mr. Megna will only
make recorlmendations and that ultimate approrJ rests with city. Undi the first test,
where the contract expressly provides for a-slgnificant amount of control and direction by
the city, which also retains the ultimate decision-making authority, the personnel of the
contracting entity do not fulfill the qualifications of a colsdtant. ioel GuercioAdvice
Letter, I-01-116.) According to your facts, it does not appear that ivlr. Megna will
perform any of the functions listed in subdivisions (g(2)(A)l-7. Thereforl, we look to
the second test to determine if Mr. Megna qualifies as aitonsultant,, under the Act.
To assist in determining if an individual is a consultant under the second test,
whether he or she "serves in a staff cap acity," the Commission has explained that the test
for serving in a staffcapacity is two-pronged. Both prongs of the test must be satisfied to
qualifr an individual as a consultant. The first p.orrg is used to identify only individuals


FileNo. A-04-067
Page No. 5
who are performing substantially all the same tasks that would usually be performed by
staff members of the govemmental agency. This eliminates the inclusion of irdirridrui,
wlto work only on a discrete project and includes the quasi-staffmember. Included in the
elements of this prong is the requirement that the individual participate in making a
governmental decision as defined in regulation 1g702.2.
You provided in your facts that Mr. Megna is performing substantially the same
duties as an economic development directorwould provide to thi city and rdevelopment
agency if that position was authorized and. filled by an ernployee of tie city, although
neither the city nor the agency's conflict of interest code dlsignates an ..economic
development consultant" as a position. If the economic development director should be
designated, in the agency's conflict of interest code because the position is one in which
the person makes orparticipates in the making of decisions, theri this first prong of the
test is met for Mr- Megna performing those same duties under contract. (ierberAdvice
Letter' No- A-98-l 18.) According to your facts, this position involves the participation
in the making of govemmental decisions as defined in regulation l87O2.Z and it should
be a designated position-
The second prong is a temporal qualifier. This is used to limit the individuals
included as consultants under the "staffcapaci$' test to those who wor:lc for the agency
for a significant amount of time. Implicit in the notion of "serves in a staffcapacity, is
that there is an on-going relationship between the contractor and the public agency. The
standard does not include individuals who work on one project or a limited range of
projects for the agency unless those projects extend oro u iong period of ttme. (Travis
Advice Letter, No. 4-96-053; Randolph Advice Letter, No. A-9S-045.) According to
your facts, this project will conclude in one year. Previous Commission advice has found
that ater]rn of more than one year is significant enough to meet this temporal qualifier,
whereas nine months ofregular and continuo,r* *ork is not enough to qualiff.- (Ferber,
suPra, and Smith Advice Letter, No- I-99-316.) However, in the tt"*iiAdvice Letter,
No. 4-02-239, an individual providing information technology services on a single
project for 12-16 months was found to not meet the staffcapatif test since the
relationship would terminate at the end of the project.
If Mr. Megna's relationship and duties with different city entities terminates at the
end of the one-year period and Mr. Megna works exclusively o, thir one project, then no
on-going relationship has been established and Mr. Megna is not a consul^tani *nder the
Act. If, however, over time the nature of the serwices that Mr. Megna performs becomes
extended beyond those discussed, or includes the same or zubstantialty ttre same duties as
would otherwise be performed by an individual holding a position ttrat is specified, or
should be specified, in the city conflict of interest code over a longer perioi of time, then
Mr' Megna would be considered a consultant. The city council should contact us for
advice if the relationship changes in nature or the city anticipates renewing the contract
with Mr. Megna on a yearly basis.


File No. A-04-067
Page No. 6
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (gt6)
322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By:
Galena West
Counsel, Legal Division
Gwje

whether Mr. Megna is a consultant under the city's conflict of interesi code.
